text:
Once upon a time, there was a little old woman named Olga who lived in an enchanted forest. Every day, Olga wandered through the dewy woods collecting flowers that could talk. She was particularly fond of tulips that sang old ballads when petalled raindrops kissed their petals.

Olga had a pet owl named Winston. Despite being nocturnal, Winston dearly loved snuggling under Olga's branches. During the busy seasons, Olga would entertain woodland creatures with music she'd learned from her tulip friends.

One unusually warm spring day, Olga ventured further than usual towards a glittering lake rumored to house unicorns. As twilight deepened, a gentle glow enveloped the water's surface. In stepped a unicorn, its horn aglow, beckoning Olga.

"Well now," the unicorn said softly, "You've heard tales of our kingdom, haven't you?"

Olga nodded, her heart fluttering.
